title: A Solo Company's Must-Have: Complete Guide to Managing Startup Projects with Feishu Bitable
excerpt: Set up your startup project management system with Feishu Bitable for free — from task tracking to data analysis, manage everything solo.
content:
One of the biggest headaches for solo founders is project management. Without a team, you have to remember everything yourself — product development progress, client follow-up status, content publishing schedule, financial data, marketing activities. Messages get scattered across WeChat groups, files live in different docs, and tasks pile up in multiple to-do apps.

Over time, this inevitably leads to problems: forgetting important things, redoing work that's already done, feeling overwhelmed by a backlog with no clear prioritization. Then the anxiety sets in — "I'm so busy, but why does it feel like I've accomplished nothing?"

The solution is right at your fingertips. Feishu Bitable is a free, powerful tool that lets you build a complete startup project management system. No coding required, no expense, and a single person can handle all the configuration.

## Why Feishu Bitable Is Perfect for Solo Companies

Before diving into specifics, let's look at why Feishu Bitable beats traditional project management tools for indie developers.

### Unmatched Flexibility

Trello, Asana, and Notion are all good tools, but they each have a "fixed view mode." Trello is kanban-only, Asana is task-list-centric, and Notion — while flexible — requires complex setup.

Feishu Bitable's "multi-dimensional" strength: the same data can be displayed in table, kanban, calendar, gallery, Gantt chart, and other views. No more switching between tools — one Bitable handles everything.

### Free Tier Is Powerful Enough

For a solo company, Feishu's free version includes most features you'll need. The per-table limits are generous for personal use, and all core features — linking, referencing, formulas, automation — don't require payment.

This is especially important in the early bootstrapping phase. When your project isn't generating steady income yet, spending limited funds on subscription tools isn't smart.

### Seamless Feishu Ecosystem Integration

If you're already using Feishu for communication and document management, Bitable integrates natively with Feishu Calendar, Docs, Meetings, and IM. You can reference table data directly in Feishu messages, embed live table views in documents, and show task deadlines on your calendar. For a solo company, this all-in-one efficiency boost is significant.

## Module 1: Task Management Kanban

Create a new Bitable named "Startup Project Management System." Then create your first sheet — "Task Management."

### Define Fields

Start with these core fields:

- **Task Name** (Text): Short description. Use "verb + noun" format, e.g. "Complete registration feature" or "Publish weekly report"
- **Project** (Single Select): Link to your project name, like "SaaS Development," "Content Operations," "Client Follow-up"
- **Priority** (Single Select): P0 — Urgent, P1 — Important, P2 — Normal, P3 — Low
- **Status** (Single Select): Not Started, In Progress, Review, Done, Shelved
- **Due Date** (Date): Task deadline
- **Est. Hours** (Number): Estimated hours needed
- **Actual Hours** (Number): Actual time spent
- **Related Docs** (Text): Links to relevant Feishu documents
- **Notes** (Text): Any additional info

### Set Up Kanban View

Add a view and select "Kanban" mode, grouped by "Status." This lets you drag and drop task cards just like Trello, giving you a visual overview of task flow.

Use color labels for priority and status. Red for urgent P0, orange for P1, blue for P2, gray for P3. Green for done — so you can instantly see which work is complete.

### Add Calendar View

Add a "Calendar" view to display tasks by due date. This gives you a weekly and monthly view of your schedule. Mark tasks due this week to easily get your weekly plan at a glance.

For solo founders, the calendar view helps you spot overloaded days and adjust before you get stuck with 10 things due on the same day and nothing to do the next.

## Module 2: Content Publishing Calendar

Create a second sheet called "Content Management" to manage your content marketing activities.

### Field Setup

- **Title** (Text): Article title
- **Type** (Single Select): Blog post, Newsletter, Social media post, Video
- **Topic Tags** (Multi Select): e.g. "SaaS Operations," "Solo Company," "Product Development"
- **Target Keyword** (Text): SEO target keyword
- **Status** (Single Select): Idea, Writing, Review, Published
- **Scheduled Date** (Date): Planned publish date
- **Actual Date** (Date): Actual publish date
- **Platforms** (Multi Select): Website, WeChat, Zhihu, Xiaohongshu
- **Performance** (Text): Views, engagement metrics post-publication

### Use Table View for Scheduling

Switch to table view, sort by "Scheduled Date," and you'll see a complete content calendar. For a solo company, content marketing needs to be consistent rather than explosive — 1–2 quality articles per week is enough. The key is consistency, not bursts.

Plan content topics for the entire month ahead, then complete one article per week. This keeps your publishing steady and prevents interruptions when you get busy.

### Link to Task Management Table

Use Bitable's "Link" feature to connect your content sheet with the task management sheet. For example, one article might link to tasks like "Write First Draft," "Create Illustrations," and "SEO Optimization." From the article's detail page, you can see all related task progress.

Linking is simple: add a "Link" type field in the content table, select the task management table as the target, and configure the linking field. Then each content row can reference corresponding task records.

## Module 3: Client Follow-Up System

Create a third sheet called "Client Management" to track prospects and paying customers.

### Field Setup

- **Client Name** (Text): Name or company name
- **Source** (Single Select): Email list, Social media, Referral, Organic search, Other
- **Status** (Single Select): Lead, First contact, Trial, Paid, Lost
- **Amount Paid** (Number): Customer's payment amount
- **First Payment Date** (Date): Date of first payment
- **Last Contact Date** (Date): Last communication date
- **Notes** (Text): Communication history and feedback

### Use Gallery View for Clients

Feishu Bitable supports a "Gallery" view that displays each record as a card. This is especially intuitive for client management — you can see each client's name, status, and last contact date at a glance.

Switch to kanban view grouped by "Status" to see your customer funnel: how many leads to follow up, how many in trial, how many are paying. This gives you a clear picture of your conversion rates.

### Automated Follow-Up Reminders

Feishu Bitable supports automation rules. Set one: "If Last Contact Date is more than 7 days ago and Status is Trial, send me a Feishu message." This way you'll never forget to follow up with important prospects.

Setting up automation is intuitive: click the "Automation" button in the top-right corner, select a trigger (e.g. "When records meet conditions"), set conditions (Last Contact Date < Today - 7 days AND Status = Trial), and choose the action (send a Feishu message to yourself).

## Module 4: Financial Data Tracking

Create a fourth sheet called "Financial Management" to record your income and expenses.

### Field Setup

- **Date** (Date): Transaction date
- **Type** (Single Select): Income, Expense
- **Category** (Single Select): SaaS subscription revenue, Consulting income, Product sales, Server costs, Tool subscriptions, Marketing expenses, etc.
- **Amount** (Number): Specific amount
- **Payment Method** (Single Select): Bank transfer, Alipay, WeChat Pay, PayPal
- **Notes** (Text): Additional explanation

### Use Formulas for Automatic Summaries

Feishu Bitable supports formula fields. Create a "Monthly Income" formula field that auto-sums income for the current month. The formula syntax is similar to Excel.

Example formula: `SUMIFS(Amount, Type, "Income", Date, ">="&DATE(YEAR(TODAY()),MONTH(TODAY()),1))`

Similarly create "Monthly Expenses" and "Net Profit" formula fields. Once you log every transaction, these formulas calculate your financial status automatically.

### Create a Monthly Review View

Add a grouped summary view grouped by month, with income and expenses summarized separately. This gives you a clear picture of monthly cash flow.

For a solo company, cash flow management is critical for survival. Your income may fluctuate wildly — high one month, low the next. Knowing your burn rate and revenue trends is the foundation for making sound business decisions.

## Module 5: Automated Workflows

Feishu Bitable's automation features are what solo founders should leverage most. They save massive amounts of repetitive work.

### Auto-Notify for Overdue Tasks

Set an automation: if a task's due date is today and its status is not "Done," trigger a Feishu message notification. You'll never miss a deadline.

In the notification config, you can customize the message — e.g. "Reminder: {Task Name} is due today. Please complete it ASAP." The notification auto-fills with your Bitable fields.

### Content Publication Automation

When content status changes from "Review" to "Published," automatically send a Feishu notification: "{Title} was published on {Platform}."

You can also integrate with a Feishu bot so that when content goes live, it automatically posts a message to your team or content group.

### Auto-Generated Weekly Reports

Use the grouping feature to quickly generate weekly report data. Group by "Completion Date," filter for the latest week, and see the number of completed tasks, published articles, new clients, and more.

Feishu Bitable also supports exporting to Excel or PDF for presentations or archiving.

## Advanced Tips

### Use Templates to Speed Up Setup

Feishu Bitable offers many preset templates for project management, client management, content management, and more. Start with a template and customize it rather than building from scratch.

Go to Bitable's "New" page, select "Template Center," search for the template you need, copy it with one click, and modify the fields.

### Cross-Table Data Linking

Different tables can be interconnected — this is Bitable's most powerful feature. For example, "Paid Client" records in the client table can link to "Client Service" tasks in the task management table. When you view a client record, you'll directly see all related service tasks.

Linked records can be viewed and edited directly from the detail page without switching contexts.

### Data Export and Analysis

Feishu Bitable supports exporting data as CSV or Excel. Use this for deeper analysis or importing into other analytics tools.

Regularly export your project data for quarterly and annual reviews. See which projects have the best ROI and which processes could be more efficient. Any review without data is just gut feeling.

## Summary

Feishu Bitable is one of the best free management tools for indie developers and solo companies. No complex code or configuration needed — it's fast to learn, highly flexible, and completely free. From task management and content planning to client follow-up and financial tracking, one tool covers it all.

For a bootstrapping solo company, time is your most valuable resource. Setting up an efficient management system might take a few hours upfront, but it saves massive time in the long run — no more jumping between multiple tools, no more worrying about forgotten tasks, no more anxiety about "what am I even doing?"

Most importantly: this system is yours. You can adjust, optimize, and expand it anytime. It adapts to how you work — not the other way around.

Go give it a try now. Open Feishu, create a Bitable, and build your first task kanban — that's the first step from "busy but chaotic" to "busy and organized." Systematic management is itself the foundation of passive income.
